summarization.a: The figure shows the cathodoluminescence (CL) intensity of a material at different wavelengths after various treatments (initial, RIE, ALE).
summarization.b: The figure shows the cathodoluminescence (CL) intensity of a material at different wavelengths after various treatments (initial, RIE, ALE).

b.question: At what wavelength is the peak CL intensity observed?
b.answer: b	The peak intensity is observed at 350 nm.

b.question: What is the CL intensity difference between RIE and ALE at the maximum intensity?
b.answer: b	The maximum difference is around 1500 counts

a.question: Which etch process has a bigger influence on the CL intensity?
a.answer: a	ALE seems to decrease the CL intensity the most, eventhough RIE also decreases the CL intensity a lot.

a.question: Why does the CL intensity change upon etching?
a.answer: a	It is likely caused by the chemically changing of the emitting material. ALE is likely the chemically change the surface, resulting in a bigger decrease in CL intensity
